title: Looking Back from the End of Your Life at the Lineage You Started
subtitle: A visualisation for seeing yourself as the turning point in a long line
excerpt: A ten-minute visualisation for Day 13 of the money arc. The scene places you at the end of a long life, looking back at the lineage you started, who came after, what they inherited, what changed because you chose differently.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Inspired by the Day 13 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796): allowing a new wealthy lineage to begin.

content.text: A visualisation for Day 13. The scene is simple: you are old, the work is done, and you are looking back at what started with you. Ten minutes is enough. Sit or lie down, close your eyes, and let the image build slowly.
You are at the end of a long life. The room around you is quiet. You are sitting, a chair, a bed, wherever feels solid.
Let the years compress behind you. Not every year, just the shape of the arc. The decisions made. The things released. The money that arrived and stayed. The money that went out and returned.

Now picture the people who came after you. Children. Grandchildren. People you never met but whose lives are shaped by what you started. What do they have that the generation before you didn't have? Not objects, the way they relate to money. The absence of a particular fear. A belief that was never installed because you removed it from the line.

Look at one specific person, a grandchild, a great-grandchild. She is sitting somewhere comfortable. She doesn't have the money panic you once had at 3am. She doesn't have the "we never get ahead" story. She inherited something different. Hold that image for a moment.

Now look back at yourself at the age you are now, the one doing the work, on Day 13. See her from this future point. She is the person who started all of it. She doesn't know yet how far it will reach.
Stay with that for a few breaths. When you are ready, open your eyes and write one sentence about what you saw.

Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 13 theme of allowing a new wealthy lineage to begin, drawn from MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). End-of-life visualisation as a way to gain perspective has roots in several contemplative traditions. These include Stoic memento mori exercises and Buddhist life-review practices.
